ops.title: Batch round.started + block event into one store transaction
ops.description: Review finding (verdict OVERSTATED/low — real non-atomicity, exceptional-failure-only): handlePush and handleUpsertBlock append round.started and the doc/block event as two independent SQLite transactions; if the second append fails (I/O, disk-full, cancellation, power loss — validation is all up front), the round has advanced without the intended change (push path: carry-less, old blocks frozen). Reducer-side carry-skip (shipped) already prevents log poisoning; the remaining gap is the partial round-advance. Proper fix needs a multi-event append API in the cc-interact substrate (one transaction), then both appends batch. Verdict record: ~/.cache/codex-ask/runs/codex-root.dc_vktdp/present-refute/codex-r-2m1itkru

ops.text: cc-interact exposes a transactional multi-append (or explicit decline with rationale)
handlePush/handleUpsertBlock/handleRound batch their two appends through it, with a failure-injection test
